steps.action: Hula () is a Hawaiian dance form expressing chan with pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: Hula () is a Hawaiian dance form expressing chan with pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: The hula dramatizes or portrays the words of the
steps.text: The hula dramatizes or portrays the words of the oli or mele in a visual dance form.

steps.action: Ancient hula, performed before Western encounter
steps.text: Ancient hula, performed before Western encounters with Hawaiʻi, is called kahiko.

steps.action: It is accompanied by chant and traditional instr
steps.text: It is accompanied by chant and traditional instruments.

steps.action: There are also two main positions of a hula danc
steps.text: There are also two main positions of a hula dance: either sitting (noho dance) or standing (luna dance).

steps.action: Some dances utilize both forms.
steps.text: Some dances utilize both forms.

steps.action: Hula dancing is complex, with many hand motions
steps.text: Hula dancing is complex, with many hand motions used to represent the words in a song or chant.

steps.action: Foot and hip movements often pull from a basic l
steps.text: Foot and hip movements often pull from a basic library of steps including the kāholo, kaʻo, kāwelu, hela, ʻuwehe, and ʻami.

steps.action: These two genres reflect the social transformati
steps.text: These two genres reflect the social transformation and westernization within the region, influenced by American economics and politics.

steps.action: More importantly, the same strophic text format
steps.text: More importantly, the same strophic text format is applied in both genres, constructed with two or four lines of text, with each of them commonly set to a uniform number of beats.

steps.action: During performance, it is a usual practice that
steps.text: During performance, it is a usual practice that the songs are separated into stanzas which are repeated by a brief rhythmic interlude.

steps.action: Many hula were created to praise the chiefs and
steps.text: Many hula were created to praise the chiefs and performed in their honor, or for their entertainment.

steps.action: Today hula kahiko is simply stated as "Tradition
steps.text: Today hula kahiko is simply stated as "Traditional" Hula.

steps.action: Many hula dances are considered to be a religiou
steps.text: Many hula dances are considered to be a religious performance, as they are dedicated to, or honoring, a Hawaiian goddess or god.

steps.action: Dancers who were learning to do such hula necess
steps.text: Dancers who were learning to do such hula necessarily made many mistakes.

steps.action: Hula kahiko is performed today by dancing to the
steps.text: Hula kahiko is performed today by dancing to the historical chants.

steps.action: Many hula kahiko are characterized by traditiona
steps.text: Many hula kahiko are characterized by traditional costuming, by an austere look, and a reverence for their spiritual root.

steps.action: Traditional chant types are extremely varied in
steps.text: Traditional chant types are extremely varied in context and technical components, and cover a broad range of specific functions.

steps.action: An important distinction between oli, hula, and
steps.text: An important distinction between oli, hula, and mele is as follows: mele can hold many different meanings, and is often translated to mean simply, song.

steps.action: Hula (chant with dance) and oli (chant without d
steps.text: Hula (chant with dance) and oli (chant without dance) are two general styles in which mele can be used/performed.

steps.action: The combination of general style (with or withou
steps.text: The combination of general style (with or without dance) and the context of the performance determines what vocal style a chant will use.

steps.action: Olioli is a style many would liken to song, as i
steps.text: Olioli is a style many would liken to song, as it is melodic in nature and includes sustained pitches, often with 'i'i, or vibrato of the voice that holds vowel tones at the ends of lines.

steps.action: As a result of Americanization, including the sp
steps.text: As a result of Americanization, including the spread of Christianity, many traditional chants became viewed as pagan and were ultimately forgotten.

steps.action: These five styles are: kepakepa: A conversationa
steps.text: These five styles are: kepakepa: A conversational patter that is expected to be performed swiftly, where the syllables are usually too short to allow pitches to be identified.
